objections.description: Using React/Babel via CDN and in-browser transpilation is unacceptable for a 'production-grade' frontend. It introduces significant runtime overhead, causes Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC), blocks main thread during parsing, and violates the 1.5s perceived-first-paint goal. This approach is development-only tech.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement a build step (Vite recommended) that compiles React/TSX to optimized static assets in src/frontend/web/dist. web_server serves the pre-built index.html and hashed assets. No runtime transpilation.

objections.description: Design tokens in tokens.css are too vague. 'Typography hierarchy doing most of the work' requires explicit definition to ensure premium aesthetic. Without strict type scales and font loading strategies, the visual quality will degrade to default browser styles during load.
objections.suggested_fix: Define explicit font-family stacks (system-ui + fallbacks), strict type scale (e.g., 12/14/16/20/24/32), and font-weight tokens (400/500/600/700) in tokens.css. Include font-display: swap strategies.

objections.description: BottomSheet and ActionSheet components lack specified accessibility behavior. Visual design critic requires focus trapping, scroll locking, and aria-modal attributes to be part of the spec to ensure visual focus states are meaningful.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly document focus management strategy (e.g., React Focus Lock) and aria-hidden state toggling for background content when sheets are open in the component spec.

suggestions.description: Migration path should include a build script in package.json that outputs directly to src/frontend/web/dist to ensure CI/CD compatibility with the existing web_server.
Define specific color contrast ratios for the dark-mode-first approach (WCAG AA minimum) within the token system to prevent accessibility debt during visual implementation.
